summary: Strangler Pattern Backend Refactor - REGRESSION TEST PASSED. All 28 tests pass. Auth routes (register, login, me, logout) migrated to routes/auth.py work correctly. Venue routes (search, get, create, update, reviews) migrated to routes/venues.py work correctly. Legacy routes (leads, payments, admin, comparison-sheet) in server.py are unaffected.

minor.issue: Test credential discrepancy - venue owner email in test request was venue1@bookmyvenue.in but actual DB has venue@bookmyvenue.in
minor.resolution: Updated test file with correct credential. This is a documentation issue, not a code bug.

action_items: Update documentation to reflect correct venue owner credential: venue@bookmyvenue.in (not venue1@bookmyvenue.in)
critical_code_review_comments: routes/auth.py: Clean implementation with proper JWT handling, password hashing, and Google OAuth session support
routes/venues.py: Well-structured with comprehensive search filters (city, area, event_type, guest count, price, amenities), pagination, and sorting
Both route files correctly import from centralized config.py, models/__init__.py, and utils/__init__.py
Code passes Python linting (ruff) with no errors
Modular router pattern correctly uses APIRouter with prefix and tags for proper OpenAPI docs
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend refactor regression test complete. Auth and Venue routes successfully migrated from server.py to modular routes/auth.py and routes/venues.py using strangler pattern. All 14 endpoints tested: (1) Auth: register, login, google-session, me, logout (2) Venues: search, get, create, update, reviews (3) Legacy: leads, lead details, admin control-room, payments. Test file: /app/backend/tests/test_strangler_refactor_regression.py
